name: Los Angeles
tagline: Sunshine, Beaches, and the Entertainment Capital of the World
description: Los Angeles offers FIRE retirees 284 days of sunshine, world-class beaches, and a creative energy found nowhere else. The sprawling metropolis demands a car, which adds cost, but rewards with incredible ethnic food diversity, hiking in the Santa Monica Mountains, and a laid-back lifestyle that makes every day feel like vacation. California's state income tax hits portfolios hard, but the weather, culture, and outdoor access create an undeniable quality of life.

quickFacts.visa: No visa required for US citizens. California has high state income tax (up to 13.3%), which significantly impacts FIRE withdrawal strategies.
quickFacts.internetSpeed: 200-1000 Mbps fiber available via AT&T and Spectrum
quickFacts.safetyRating: Moderately Safe — varies widely by neighborhood; many areas are very safe with typical big-city awareness needed
quickFacts.nearestAirport: Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) — 25 min from downtown; Burbank (BUR) — 20 min from Hollywood
quickFacts.climate: Mediterranean with dry, warm summers (25-35°C) and mild, occasionally wet winters (10-18°C). Sunshine 284 days per year.

housing.description: Small 1-bedroom apartment in Koreatown, East Hollywood, or the San Fernando Valley — livable but far from the beach

dining.description: Incredible taco trucks, Korean BBQ in K-Town, Thai Town noodle spots, and pho in the SGV — LA's cheap eats rival any city on earth

healthcare.description: Covered California bronze plan, community clinic visits for routine care

transportation.description: Older reliable car with insurance and gas — a car is essentially mandatory in LA. Metro for occasional trips

entertainment.description: Free beach days in Malibu and Venice, hiking Runyon Canyon and Griffith Park, free museum days at the Getty and Broad

1M.lifestyle: A modest apartment in Koreatown or the Valley, well away from the beach. You need a car, which eats into an already tight budget, and California's high state income tax reduces your effective spending power. The tradeoff is year-round sunshine, excellent cheap ethnic food, and free access to beaches and hiking trails. You cook most meals at home and keep entertainment costs low by taking advantage of the outdoors.

housing.description: Nice 1-bedroom in Silver Lake, Mar Vista, or a studio in Santa Monica — walkable neighborhoods with personality and some coastal proximity

dining.description: Brunch at Sqirl, dinners at neighborhood favorites in Silver Lake and Echo Park, regular sushi, farmers market meals on weekends

healthcare.description: Covered California silver plan with access to Cedars-Sinai or UCLA Health network, dental and vision included

transportation.description: Reliable car with insurance, gas, and parking. Occasional Lyft for nights out, e-bike for neighborhood errands

entertainment.description: Surf lessons in Malibu, Hollywood Bowl season under the stars, LACMA membership, comedy at the Laugh Factory, Dodgers games

2M.lifestyle: A nice one-bedroom in Silver Lake or Mar Vista, with some coastal proximity. You eat out several times a week at good mid-range restaurants, visit the farmers market on weekends, and catch shows at the Hollywood Bowl in summer. A reliable car with insurance is a fixed cost. California income tax takes a noticeable bite, but you live well, with regular beach days and enough budget for occasional trips up the coast.

housing.description: Spacious 2-bedroom in West Hollywood, a bungalow in Venice, or a modern apartment in Santa Monica with ocean breezes

dining.description: Regular dinners at Bestia, Gjelina, and Providence. Omakase at Sushi Ginza Onodera, wine tastings in Malibu Canyon vineyards

healthcare.description: Premium PPO with Cedars-Sinai or UCLA Health, concierge primary care, annual executive physical, full dental

transportation.description: Leased mid-range car (Tesla Model 3 or similar), premium insurance, weekend road trips up the PCH to Santa Barbara

entertainment.description: VIP Hollywood Bowl box, surfboard and wetsuit collection, Lakers or Rams games, private yoga sessions, weekend wine country trips

3M.lifestyle: A spacious apartment in West Hollywood or Santa Monica, or a small house in Venice. You dine regularly at well-regarded restaurants, drive a decent car, and take weekend trips up the PCH to Santa Barbara or out to Joshua Tree. At $10,000 a month you are living comfortably in a good neighborhood with real breathing room for entertainment and travel. California taxes remain a factor, but the quality of life is high.

housing.description: Modern architectural home in the Hollywood Hills, beachfront apartment in Malibu, or a luxury condo in Century City with skyline views

dining.description: Private chef for weeknight meals, regular Nobu and n/naka omakase, exclusive wine dinners, hosted poolside dinner parties

healthcare.description: Concierge medicine at UCLA Executive Health or Cedars-Sinai VIP, longevity clinic membership, full wellness team

transportation.description: Leased luxury vehicle (Porsche Cayenne or Tesla Model X), premium parking, occasional private car service for events

entertainment.description: Soho House membership, courtside Lakers seats occasionally, private surf instructor, Coachella VIP, film premiere invitations

domesticHelp.description: Weekly housekeeper, pool maintenance, gardener and landscaping for the property

luxuryExtras.description: Personal trainer, private Pilates, wardrobe stylist, premium beach club membership, annual Coachella and film festival VIP passes

5M.lifestyle: A large home in the Hollywood Hills or a luxury apartment in Century City, with a nice car for getting around. You eat at the city's best restaurants regularly, have a housekeeper come weekly, and take weekend trips to Palm Springs or Malibu without budgeting for them. Business class flights for longer travel are comfortable at this budget. California's 13.3% top income tax rate is the main drawback, but the day-to-day lifestyle is genuinely affluent.

housing.description: Iconic estate in Bel Air, Brentwood, or beachfront compound in Malibu with pool, guest house, and panoramic ocean or canyon views

dining.description: Full-time private chef with farm-to-table sourcing, curated wine cellar, exclusive private dining clubs, hosted charity dinners for 50

healthcare.description: Elite concierge medical team, anti-aging and longevity protocols, fly-anywhere specialist access, private wellness retreats

transportation.description: Chauffeured luxury vehicle, exotic car collection, private jet charter for Aspen or Cabo weekends, helicopter to avoid the 405

entertainment.description: Film production investments, Oscar after-party circuit, philanthropic galas, private art collection, courtside season tickets for Lakers

domesticHelp.description: Full estate staff: house manager, private chef, housekeeper, gardener/pool technician, personal assistant, and security

luxuryExtras.description: Interior designer on retainer, bespoke wardrobe curation, art advisor, charitable foundation, Malibu vineyard investment, wellness concierge

10M.lifestyle: A large home in Bel Air, Brentwood, or beachfront in Malibu with a pool and guest house. You have a housekeeper, a cook who comes in several times a week, and a nice car. You dine at top restaurants frequently, travel first class, and support local arts institutions as a donor. This is comfortable, established wealth in a city where the weather and outdoor access are hard to beat. Even at this budget, California taxes take a significant share.
